text: Universal Approximation Theorem: a single hidden layer can approximate any continuous function

Neural networks are computing systems inspired by biological neurons: layers of interconnected nodes that transform input data through weighted connections and activation functions. A simple feedforward network: Input Layer → Hidden Layer(s) → Output Layer. Each neuron computes weighted sum + bias + activation function.
Activation functions: ReLU (most common), sigmoid (binary out), tanh (-1 to 1), softmax (multi-class probability).
Universal Approximation Theorem: a single hidden layer can approximate any continuous function (given enough neurons).
